% c.2 effort-break: the two pseudofunctoriality sub-lemmas (a) + (b)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

\begin{lemma}[Cone B / c.2(a): cocycle law of the adjunction-uniqueness iso \(H1\) over the immersion factorisation]
  \leanok
  \label{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualH1Cocycle}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv, lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans, lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be composable open immersions, write
  \(\varphi_g = g.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}.\mathrm{hom}\), and let
  \[
    H1_g \;=\; (\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\,\cdots).\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,
      (\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi_g)
      \;\colon\; \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_g
  \]
  be the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism re-presenting the presheaf pullback as the pushforward
  (Stage~H1 of \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}). Then \(H1\) satisfies a \emph{cocycle}
  (composition) law over the factorisation \(h \mathbin{;} f\): on the pullback (source) flank it is
  threaded by the presheaf pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\)
  (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}) and on the pushforward (target) flank by the
  restriction-composition iso \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}, equivalently the pushforward composition
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\)). Writing \(FC = \mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) for the left-adjoint
  composition isomorphism of the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\), reconciled by the presheaf
  pushforward composition \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\), the realized cocycle is
  the \emph{hom}-form identity
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_f).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f \,\triangleleft\, (H1_h).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom} ,
  \]
  stated as a faithful \(\forall\)-strengthening over the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\)
  (\(g \in \{f,\, h,\, h\mathbin{;}f\}\)); the consumer instantiates it with the matching
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) adjunctions. Here \(\triangleright\) is right-whiskering
  (\(\alpha \triangleright F = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,F\)) and \(\triangleleft\) left-whiskering
  (\(F \triangleleft \alpha = \mathtt{whiskerLeft}\,F\,\alpha\)). Inverting the identity (swap \(f\)
  and \(h\) in the two middle legs and replace \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom}\)
  by its \(\mathrm{inv}\)) yields the form in which \(\theta\)'s \(H1\)-prefix is recombined inside
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
  This is the dual-flank analogue of \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv}
  (\(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\)) and of
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}). This is the \(H1\)-flank of the cocycle
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv, lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans, lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \leanok
  Each \(H1_g\) is a \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison of two left adjoints of the shared right
  adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}\). The composite-immersion comparison \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)
  decomposes by transitivity of \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\)
  (\cref{lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans}) once the composite pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) is identified with the iterated pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_h \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f\) through
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\) (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}), and the
  composite pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) with the iterated pushforward
  through \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\) (equivalently \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\),
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}). Because \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) is \emph{defined} as
  the left-adjoint composition isomorphism \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\), the reconciliation of these
  two pseudofunctor isos on the conjugate is exactly \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv},
  instantiated at the present adjunctions; substituting it collapses the conjugate to the displayed
  three-factor chain. Concretely, this is a direct instantiation of the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the three pushforward/pullback adjunction pairs,
  with \(F_\bullet = \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), \(P_\bullet = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the
  shared composition iso \(e = \mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\); the \(P\)-side
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) is definitionally \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Generic \(H1\)-cancellation skeleton for the c.2 cocycle]
  \label{lem:c2_assemble}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.c2_assemble}
  \uses{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Work in an arbitrary category \(C\) with objects \(A_1, \dots, A_9\) and the morphisms appearing in
  the c.2 reduction. Given the eight structural facts of that reduction --- the two iso cancellations
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)) and
  \(\mathtt{fcinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{fc} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(FC\)), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pc}\)
  (instantiating \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}), the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) cancellation
  \(\mathtt{pc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p0} = \mathbf{1}\), the single \(H1_h.\mathrm{hom}\) naturality
  slide \(\mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} = \mathtt{Pushhif} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hhdmf}\), the
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\)-functoriality fold
  \(\mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pushhif} = \mathtt{pushSDTf}\), the \(H1_h\) round-trip
  \(\mathtt{hhdmf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Hhinv} = \mathbf{1}\), and the residual \((**)\)
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s} = \mathtt{pushSDTf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  --- the c.2 goal identity
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}
    = \mathtt{p0} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Hhinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  holds. This is the instance-agnostic engine that performs the entire \(H1\)-cancellation of
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}; the consumer supplies its eight hypotheses at
  the concrete pushforward/pullback morphisms (the cocycle hypothesis being
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, itself \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the
  three pushforward adjunctions, reconciled by \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \leanok
  Pure associativity and identity rewriting. Pre-compose the cocycle goal with \(\mathtt{fc}\) and use
  the cocycle hypothesis together with the residual \((**)\); the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
  cancellation, the \(H1_h\) naturality slide, the \(H1_h\) round-trip and the functoriality fold then
  collapse the middle in turn, yielding \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} (\text{RHS}) = \mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}\).
  Cancelling the iso \(\mathtt{fc}\) (via \(\mathtt{fcinv}\)) and then the iso \(\mathtt{aH}\) (via
  \(\mathtt{aHinv}\)) gives the stated identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Bridge dual-(c): cocycle law of the presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\) over \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)]
  \leanok
  \label{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualPullbackComparison_restrict}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app, lem:slice_dual_transport_comp, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:c2_assemble, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_counit_app_app_apply}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism, \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) satisfies a \emph{cocycle} (composition) law over the
  immersion factorisation, in the \emph{exact dual shape} of the tensor composition coherence
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}: the \(\iota_V\)-instance \(\theta_{\iota_V, M}\) factors
  through the \(j\)-pullback of the \(\iota_U\)-instance and the \(j\)-instance \emph{at the pushforward
  object} \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) --- the
  base-change image of \(M_{\mathrm{val}}\), \emph{not} the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) ---
  conjugated by the presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) on the
  source and by its contravariant transport on the dual target:
  \[
    \theta_{\iota_V, M}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{inv}_{\,\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\bigl(\theta_{\iota_U, M}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\bigl(\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\,
          ((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M)\bigr).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Stating the middle factor at the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) is type-correct because the
  object-identification iso \(\mathtt{pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}\)
  (\cref{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}, bridge c.1) is \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\): the underlying
  presheaf \((M|_{\iota_U})_{\mathrm{val}}\) is \emph{definitionally} the pushforward object
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) (the presheaf-level pushforward
  composition being the identity), so the codomain of \((\mathtt{pullback}\,j)(\theta_{\iota_U, M})\)
  --- namely
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_j).\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\mathtt{dual}((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\bigr)\)
  --- already matches the domain of \(\theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}\) with no intervening transport. This is
  the form consumed by \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}. On the dual (target) flank the
  contravariant transport is realised by \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of the restriction-composition iso
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}); this pseudofunctoriality leg appears in place of the
  \(\varrho \otimes \varrho\) of the tensor side. This is the dual analogue of the composition role of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}.

  one isolated pushforward-flank identity that touches \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\)'s internals.

  \(dM = \mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\),

  \(FC = \mathtt{pushforwardComp}\), and
  \(\varrho = (\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\), and expand each instance via
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison} as
  \(\theta_g = (H1_g)^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_g\). The reduction runs in six steps.
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \textbf{Strip the leading inverse.} Left-multiply both sides by
      \(H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}\) (using the isomorphism inverse-composition identity); the left side
      becomes \(\mathtt{sDT}_{\iota_V}\) and the right side acquires the prefix
      \(H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}\).
    \item \textbf{Expose the cocycle prefix.} Left-multiply by the \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) counit
      \(FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\), legitimate because it is an isomorphism (hence an epimorphism); the
      left flank now reads \(FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM} \mathbin{;} H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\).
    \item \textbf{Substitute cocycle (a).} By the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
      \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle} this prefix equals
      \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_j).\mathrm{map}\,(H1_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom})
        \mathbin{;} H1_j.\mathrm{hom}_{(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U})\,dM}
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\),
      regrouping the \(H1_{\iota_U}\) factor across the pushforward middle functor on the source flank
      (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\), \cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}).
    \item \textbf{Cancel \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\).} The trailing
      \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\) meets the leading
      \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{inv}_{dM}\) of the right side and the two cancel
      (as the hom-inv identity of an isomorphism).

      \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_j).\mathrm{map}\,(\theta_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom})\) by a \emph{single}
      ordinary naturality square of the natural transformation \(H1_j.\mathrm{hom}\) at
      \(\theta_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom}\) --- this is the only ``slide'', and it is plain
      naturality of \(H1_j.\mathrm{hom}\) as a natural transformation, not a bespoke multi-step calculation.

      \(H1_{\iota_U}\) and \(H1_j\) annihilate the remaining \(H1\) factors, leaving the sole residual

          \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_j(M|_{\iota_U})

  The residual \((**)\) is the pushforward-flank pseudofunctoriality of
  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\) --- the only step that touches the transport's internals --- and it
  plays the dual role of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside the proven tensor analogue

  an explicit unit/counit composite; the residual \((**)\) is reachable via this formula.

  \cref{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}. (The mate characterisation
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom} is an alternative reading --- it identifies the
  conjugate \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,FC.\mathrm{hom} = e.\mathrm{inv} = \mathbf 1\), but delivers only
  the mate, not the section-level component directly.) After

  coherence}. The leading \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) is handled sectionwise via \cref{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}: it is the composite of the pushforward adjunction's
  unit and two counits, and \emph{each} of these, evaluated at a section, is --- by the triangle-component
  identities \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply} and
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_counit_app_app_apply} --- precisely a presheaf restriction map
  \(X.\mathrm{presheaf}.\mathrm{map}(-)^{\mathrm{op}}\) of an \(\mathrm{Opens}\,X\) morphism. This form is
  reached \emph{definitionally} (the units/counits are restriction maps on the nose); direct unfolding
  of \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) would reintroduce the
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) carrier diamond. Folding the three restriction maps by functoriality
  yields one \(X.\mathrm{presheaf}.\mathrm{map}(\text{composite})^{\mathrm{op}}\); the two resulting
  slice objects share domain (\((j\mathbin{;}\iota_U).\mathtt{opensFunctor} =

  iso cancellations of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) and \(FC\) at \(dM\), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle~(a)

  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification}(\theta)\) is confined. The proof is the dual-flank mirror of S2,
  routed through Cone~B (internal-hom base-change), not through any sheaf-level monoidal structure.

  Specialise L1 (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_comp}) at \((f, g) = (j, \iota_U)\). The equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\(hjι\), \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) converts the
  \(\iota_V\)-leg \(D(\iota_V)\) on the left of the target into the composite leg \(D(j\mathbin{;}\iota_U)\)
  via L2 (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_pullbackcongr}). Combining the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hjι\)
  factor that L2 produces with the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) head of L1 reconstitutes
  \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\), which is by definition
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hjι)^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U\)
  (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}), giving the head \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,M}\). On the dual flank the
  two contravariant \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) transports fuse, by contravariant functoriality
  (\cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans}, \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}), into the single factor
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_M\). This gives the displayed square. The trivialisation
  step's \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\) transport and the datum refinement are not part of this
  square; they telescope in the parent (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat}).
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}
[Oplax-monoidal unit map $\eta$ of the presheaf pullback --- conceptual anchor]
  \label{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_eta}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η}
  \mathlibok
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any oplax monoidal functor \(F\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\eta_F : F(\mathbf 1) \to \mathbf 1\), the oplax counterpart of the unit map. Applied to the
  oplax-monoidal presheaf pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) along the chart immersion
  \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\), \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), it furnishes the bare
  oplax unit comparison
  \[
    \eta(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi) :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \longrightarrow \mathbf 1_V .
  \]
  This is the \emph{conceptual} pullback-flank unit map. Its invertibility is \emph{not} asserted
  here. The actual pullback-side comparator \(p\) used in the dual base-change theory is built
  instead as
  the H1 transport of the pushforward comparator \(q\); see
  \cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Pullback-side unit comparator $p$ --- the H1 realization]
  \label{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPullbackUnitIso}
  \uses{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion, \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\)
  the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding (sectionwise-bijective) pushforward ring
  map. The pullback-side unit comparator used throughout the dual base-change theory is the
  isomorphism
  \[
    p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  realized \emph{not} as the bare oplax \(\eta\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_eta}) but through
  the \emph{H1 reconciliation}. Both \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the open-immersion
  pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) are left adjoints of the same right adjoint
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\); the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism
  \[
    H_1 := \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\bigl(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj},\,
      \mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi\bigr) :
      \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
  \]
  identifies the two left adjoints (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app},
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply}). Evaluated at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1_U\), it carries the pullback flank to the pushforward flank, on which the pushforward
  comparator \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso})
  contracts to \(\mathbf 1_V\). Composing,
  \[
    p \;=\; (H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1} \,\mathbin{;}\, q ,
  \]
  the H1-transport of \(q\). This is exactly the identification by which the two flank comparators
  are matched at the structure-sheaf unit in the \(\theta\)-at-unit bridge
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}
[Lax-monoidal unit map $\varepsilon$ of the presheaf pushforward --- conceptual anchor]
  \label{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_eps}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε}
  \mathlibok
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any lax monoidal functor \(G\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\varepsilon_G : \mathbf 1 \to G(\mathbf 1)\). Applied to the lax-monoidal presheaf pushforward
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\beta\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}) along
  the chart immersion, it gives the bare lax unit comparison
  \[
    \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
      \mathbf 1_V \longrightarrow (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U ,
  \]
  the presheaf analogue of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\). This is the
  \emph{conceptual} pushforward-flank unit map; its invertibility is supplied not by the lax
  structure but by the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) for an open immersion
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}), and the resulting comparator \(q\) is recorded in
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Strong monoidality of the open-immersion presheaf pushforward $\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta$]
  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardBetaMonoidal}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal, lem:restrictscalars_monoidal_of_bijective}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be an open immersion and \(\beta = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,
  (\mathtt{forget}_2)\) the induced structure-ring comparison, where
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,U)^{-1}\). Since \(f\) is an open immersion each
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U\) is invertible, hence \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective. Consequently the
  presheaf pushforward factors as the strong-monoidal composite
  \[
    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;=\;
      \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta ,
  \]
  in which the topological pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\) is strong monoidal
  and \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) is strong monoidal because \(\beta\) is sectionwise
  bijective (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\)). Hence \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  carries a \emph{strong}-monoidal structure, upgrading the ambient lax instance
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}); in particular its unit comparator
  \(\varepsilon\) and tensorator \(\mu\) are isomorphisms.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Pushforward-side unit comparator $q$ --- the strong-monoidal $\varepsilon$ inverse]
  \label{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardUnitIso}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting above, the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}) makes the unit comparator an isomorphism
  \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
    \mathbf 1_V \cong (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U\). The pushforward-side
  unit comparator is its inverse,
  \[
    q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  the strong-monoidal \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}\) inverse (not the bare lax \(\varepsilon\) of
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_eps}).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator: $q$ is the $f.\mathtt{appIso}$-conjugation on carriers]
  \label{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardBetaUnitEpsAppOne}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse, lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  This is the unit-side analogue of the tensorator collapse
  \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse}. With \(f : Y \to X\) an open immersion,
  \(\beta\) the (sectionwise-bijective) structure-ring comparison, and
  \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the sectionwise carrier action of
  \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1}\) at an
  \emph{arbitrary} carrier element \(y\) is, over a section \(V\),
  \[
    \bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y)
      \;=\; (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y) ,
  \]
  the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). The unit case \(y = 1\) is the motivating
  instance; the general form is the one the L1\('\) consumer
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural} requires (it instantiates at a non-unit
  carrier).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse, lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \leanok
  We compute the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) at an arbitrary carrier element \(y\) by splitting the strong-monoidal structure of
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) along the factorization
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta = \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\,
  \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) furnished by
  \cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}. The argument is cleanest run on the \emph{inverse}
  \(q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\): under \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.comp}\)
  the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of the composite is the composite of the two factors'
  \(\varepsilon\)'s, and the scalar-forgetting pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\)
  has \(\varepsilon = \mathrm{id}\), so \(q.\mathrm{inv}\) reduces to the \(\varepsilon\)-value of
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\). That value is the sectionwise structure-ring transport
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\), by the abstract \(\varepsilon\)-template
  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app} (the strong-monoidal companion of the oplax
  \(\eta\)-template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; \(\varepsilon = \eta^{-1}\)
  since \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective). Hence
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso_inv_app}). Now both \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V(y)\) and
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y)\) are sent to \(y\) by the injective
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V\) — the former by the round-trip \(q.\mathrm{hom}\,;q.\mathrm{inv} =
  \mathrm{id}\), the latter by \(\mathtt{appIso}\)'s hom-inv identity — so they are equal:
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y)\), the
  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). This isolates the same structure-ring transport that the
  tensorator collapse \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse} isolates on the \(\mu\)-side.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Abstract $\varepsilon$-value of $\mathtt{restrictScalars}$ on carriers]
  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictScalars_laxMonoidal_ε_app}
  \uses{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  For ring-presheaves \(R, S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and a natural
  transformation \(\alpha : R \to S\), the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) acts sectionwise on carriers by the ring map
  \(\alpha\): over a section \(W\), \(\varepsilon.\mathrm{app}\,W\,(r) = (\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\).
  This is the abstract \(\{R,S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\}\) companion of the
  unit-value template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; the abstract framing makes
  the ambient \(\mathtt{CommRing}\) instance native (the concrete post-\(\mathtt{forget}_2\) Scheme
  carrier exposes only \(\mathtt{Ring}\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \leanok
  Unfold \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\)'s lax-monoidal \(\varepsilon\): it is, by construction,
  the scalar-action map induced by \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W\) on the unit module, i.e. multiplication by
  \((\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\). Reading off its value at a carrier element gives the
  claim directly.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator inverse $q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon$]
  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso_inv_app}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardUnitIso_inv_app}
  \uses{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting, with \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse \(q.\mathrm{inv} =
  \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\) acts sectionwise on carriers as the inverse structure-ring
  transport: over a section \(V\),
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\).
  This is the \(\varepsilon\)-side companion of \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}; it is
  consumed both inside that lemma and directly by the L1\('\) flank
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app}
  \leanok
  Split \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta = \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \circ
  \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}). Under
  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.comp}\) the composite \(\varepsilon\) is the composite of the factors';
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\) contributes \(\varepsilon = \mathrm{id}\), leaving the
  \(\varepsilon\)-value of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\), which by
  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app} is \((\beta.\mathrm{app}\,V)\)-multiplication, i.e.
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\) on carriers. Hence
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[S4a / L1$'$: $\theta$-at-unit bridge --- the presheaf dual base-change comparison at the structure sheaf is the dual-of-unit iso]
  \label{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualUnitIso_pullback_natural}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, lem:internal_hom_eval, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one, lem:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso_inv_app, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion with \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\),
  \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\) the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding
  pushforward ring map. Evaluate the presheaf dual base-change comparison
  \(\theta_M = \mathtt{presheafDualPullbackComparison}\,j\,M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(M = \mathbf 1_U = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,U.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\). Because \(\theta\) lands
  the dual on the pushforward flank, this gives
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1} : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U\bigr).
  \]
  Two \emph{distinct} unit comparators enter, one per flank:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the pullback-side comparator
      \(p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} q\) of the pushforward comparator;
    \item the pushforward-side comparator
      \(q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
        (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse of the strong-monoidal unit comparator
      of the pushforward.
  \end{itemize}
  Modulo the eval-at-\(1\) dual-of-unit isomorphism \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) on the \(U\)- and \(V\)-flanks, \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\)
  \emph{is} the dual-of-unit identification, with the two comparators on opposite flanks: \(q\) is
  transported contravariantly through \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) onto the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\), while \(p\) closes the pullback domain on the right:
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\; \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, p,
  \]
  both sides being isomorphisms
  \(\mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U) \cong \mathbf 1_V\). This is the
  unit-specialisation of the immersion-naturality \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  and the immersion analogue of the unit-automorphism core
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, lem:internal_hom_eval, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}
  \leanok
  By element extensionality on dual sections, exactly as in
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}, of which this is the unit specialisation. Both
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) and \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) are morphisms of presheaves of modules:
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) is the internal-hom base-change comparison
  \(f^{*}\mathcal{H}om(\mathbf 1, \mathcal{O}) \to \mathcal{H}om(f^{*}\mathbf 1, f^{*}\mathcal{O})\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}), and \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) is the
  sectionwise evaluation-at-\(1\) of the internal hom of the unit into itself
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}). Testing the displayed equality against an arbitrary dual
  section reduces, as in \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}, to the built-in
  naturality of \(\theta\) (its \(\mathtt{.hom}\) naturality), now read through the eval-at-\(1\)
  identification, where the internal-hom evaluation (\cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}) collapses both
  composites to evaluation of the pulled-back section at the image of \(1\).

  The two flanks carry the two \emph{different} comparators. On the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) the comparator \(q = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}) is transported contravariantly by
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\); on the pullback domain the comparator
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}) closes the
  square. Because \(p\) is, by definition, the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} q\), the two leading H1 factors --- the one hidden inside \(p\) and the one produced by
  unfolding \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) at the dual unit --- are telescoped away by the naturality of the
  natural isomorphism \(H_1 : \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi\) at the dual-unit contraction \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\)
  (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}). After this cancellation the entire claim reduces to a
  single \emph{pushforward-flank} sectionwise identity ($\dagger$): writing \(\mathtt{sDT}\) for the
  slice-dual transport \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,\mathbf 1_U\),
  \[
    \mathtt{sDT}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\;
    (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, q .
  \]

  This identity is proved sectionwise, as the immersion analogue of
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}. Fix a section \(V\) and a dual section \(\varphi\).
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Left-hand side.} The verified forward transport formula
      (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}) evaluates \(\mathtt{sDT}\) at the terminal slice as
      the reindexing across \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) and the
      dual-unit ring swap; precomposing by \(q\) and evaluating at \(1\) (the dual-unit iso
      \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} = \mathtt{evalLin}\,(\cdot)\,1\),
      \cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) exposes \(\mathtt{evalLin}\,\mathbf 1_U\,(\mathrm{op}\,fV)\,
      \varphi\,1\) conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) and the dual-unit ring swap.
    \item \emph{Right-hand side.} The pushforward of the eval-at-\(1\) contraction
      \((\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U)\) followed by the unit comparator
      \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) is reduced by the unit-value sub-lemma
      \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}: the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) at \(1\)
      is the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(1\), so the right side reduces to the same
      \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugated \(\mathtt{evalLin}\,\mathbf 1_U\,(\mathrm{op}\,fV)\,\varphi\,1\).
  \end{itemize}
  Both sides being the same eval-at-\(1\) value conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), equality is the
  commutativity of \(\mathcal{O}_V\)-linear endomorphisms of the (commutative) structure ring --- the
  same \(\mathtt{linearEndo\_apply\_comm}\)-style step that closes

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[S4a / L2$'$: unit assembly --- the dual-restriction comparison at the unit intertwines the dual-unit contractions]

  Drop both sides to their underlying morphisms (\(\mathtt{Iso.ext}\)) and flatten every
  \(\mathbin{;}\), \(\mathrm{mapIso}\) and inverse into a single right-associated composite. Expanding
  the bare comparison \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)\) by the dual-B1 factorisation
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}) into the three-factor composite
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification}(\theta_{\mathbf 1})\), and each contraction by its definition

  This flattened identity is discharged by a verified \emph{category-algebra assembly}: an abstract
  reassociation lemma over a bare category, proven by associativity bookkeeping alone, that crosses
  the \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,\mathbin{;}\) seam without any unfolding. The assembly splits the
  left-hand unit-restriction map \(m\) as a prefix \(m_{\mathrm p}\) followed by a core
  \(m_{\mathrm c}\) and consumes four hypotheses --- a split identity, a flank identity, an
  scp-prefix identity, and a tail identity. The split, scp-prefix and tail data are folded
  \(\mathbin{;}\)-bookkeeping (the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback} \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) prefix is matched, exactly as in S3, by the composition law of
  bridge~(b), \cref{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict}, threading the equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}; and the
  sheafification-counit naturality moves the counits past the restriction while contravariant

  identification supplied by L1$'$ (\cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}) is consumed

  reconciliations:

  \begin{itemize}

      forgotten sheaf-level unit-restriction comparator equals the presheaf pushforward-unit
      comparator \(q\), which discharges the dual-flank leg of the assembly;

      reduced-image/SCP-naturality and the common-prefix cancel, the residual is unit-only --- no
      dual factor remains.

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[Naturality of the left unitor (Cone A coverage)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor_naturality}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  For a morphism \(g : M \to N\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the left unitor
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) is natural:
  \[
    (\mathbf 1_{\mathcal{O}_X} \otimes g) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,N).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} g .
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  The left unitor is the sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor composed with the
  sheafification counit; both are natural transformations, so the square commutes by naturality of
  each leg.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[$\eta$ mate-identification: the unit iso is the sheafified presheaf unit comparison (Cone A)]
  \label{lem:pullback_unit_iso_eq_sheafify_eta}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_eq_sheafify_eta}
  \uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, def:pullback_val_iso}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\varphi'\) its associated change-of-rings map.
  The sheaf-level unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}) is the
  sheafification transport of the presheaf oplax unit comparison
  \(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}):
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}) reconciles the sheaf-level pullback
  value with the sheafified presheaf pullback and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) --- the unit-specific
  sheafification reconciliation isomorphism --- closes the unit (\(\mathbf 1\)) seam.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, def:pullback_val_iso}
  \leanok
  This is the unit square already isolated in the proof that the tensor comparison on the unit pair is
  an isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}): there the right-hand composite is shown to
  equal \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,\varphi\), which by definition is
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}). Reading that square as a defining
  equation for the sheafified \(\eta\) yields the displayed identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[$\delta$ mate-identification: the tensor comparison is the sheafified cotensorator (Cone A)]
  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorMap_eq_sheafify_delta}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, def:pullback_val_iso, def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The sheaf-level tensor comparison
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,M\,N\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) is the sheafification
  transport of the presheaf oplax cotensorator
  \(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\,N_{\mathrm{val}}\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}):
  \[
    \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,M\,N
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(M \otimes_X N)).\mathrm{inv}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\,N_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \beta_{M,N},
  \]
  where \(\beta_{M,N}\) is the tensor-of-\(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reconciliation
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}, \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) on the \(\delta\)-codomain,
  identifying the \(a_Y\)-image of the presheaf tensor with \(f^{*}M \otimes_Y f^{*}N\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, def:pullback_val_iso}
  \leanok
  This is the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}):
  the sheaf comparison was constructed precisely as the sheafified presheaf cotensorator, pre- and
  post-composed with the \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reconciliations on source and target. The stated
  equation is that construction read back.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[Cone A sub-lemma 1: \texttt{pullbackValIso} naturality at the sheaf left unitor (RHS reconciliation)]
  \label{lem:pullback_val_iso_naturality_left_unitor}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackValIso_naturality_leftUnitor}
  \uses{def:pullback_val_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); write
  \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback, \(a_Y\) for the
  sheafification functor on \(Y\), and \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\)
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}). The reconciliation isomorphism \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) is
  natural; instantiated against the sheaf-level left unitor
  \((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \to M\), its naturality
  square identifies the \(f^{*}\)-image of that unitor --- the right-hand side of the identity of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality} --- as the \(a_Y\)-sheafification of the
  \(F\)-image of the unitor's underlying presheaf morphism, conjugated by \(\mathtt{pbv}\) on source
  and target:
  \[
    (\mathtt{pbv}_{\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} f^{*}\bigl((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \((-)_{\mathrm{val}}\) denotes the underlying presheaf morphism (the forget-image) of a sheaf
  morphism.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pullback_val_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  Object-wise, \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) is the inverse \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
  comparison post-composed with the \(f^{*}\)-image of the sheafification counit
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}); both legs are natural transformations. Hence the displayed equation
  is precisely the naturality of \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) against the sheaf morphism
  \((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \to M\). Unfolding the
  sheaf-level unitor as the sheafified presheaf unitor \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,\lambda\) followed by the
  counit (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) and cancelling that counit against the counit leg inside
  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reduces the square to the naturality of the
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) comparison, which is structural.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Localization-monoidal left-unitor collapse]
  \label{lem:localization_monoidal_left_unitor_hom_app}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.leftUnitor_hom_app}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for a monoidal morphism property
  \(W\), so that \(\mathcal{D} = \mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) carries the transported
  (Gabriel--Zisman / Day-style) monoidal structure, with unit comparison \(\varepsilon\) and tensor
  comparison \(\mu_{X,Y} : L X \otimes L Y \cong L(X \otimes Y)\). For every object \(Y\) of
  \(\mathcal{C}\), the localized left unitor at the image object \(L Y\) collapses to the \(L\)-image of
  the source left unitor, modulo the unit and tensor comparisons:
  \[
    (\lambda_{L Y}).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd L Y
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,Y}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} L.\mathrm{map}\,(\lambda_Y).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1\) is the source tensor unit. The proof is definitional: the localized left unitor
  is constructed as a \(\mathtt{Localization.liftNatIso}\) of the source unitor, so on an image object it
  reduces by \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} to the source datum conjugated by the lifting
  isomorphisms, with no further coherence obligations.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Evaluation of a lifted natural transformation on an image object]
  \label{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Localization.liftNatTrans_app}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for \(W\), let \(F_1, F_2 :
  \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{E}\) be functors that lift to \(F_1', F_2' : \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{E}\)
  along \(L\), and let \(\tau : F_1 \to F_2\). The unique lifted natural transformation
  \(\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau : F_1' \to F_2'\), evaluated at an image object \(L X\), is the source
  datum conjugated by the lifting isomorphisms:
  \[
    (\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau)_{L X}
      = (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_1')_{X}.\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} \tau_X
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_2')_{X}.\mathrm{inv}.
  \]
  Thus a coherence between lifted transformations, tested on objects in the image of \(L\), reduces
  definitionally to the corresponding coherence of the source data pinned by the lifting iso --- the
  mechanism underlying the localization-monoidal unitor collapse above.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The sheafification of presheaves of modules is a localization]
  \label{lem:presheaf_sheafification_is_localization}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\alpha : R_0 \to R.\mathrm{obj}\) be the comparison from a presheaf of rings to its
  sheafification (locally injective and locally surjective). The sheafification reflector
  \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\,\alpha : \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R_0 \to
  \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,R\) is a localization functor for the morphism class
  \(W = J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0)\) of presheaf morphisms inverted by
  sheafification. Consequently the entire \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal}\) API ---
  \cref{lem:localization_monoidal_left_unitor_hom_app} and
  \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} in particular --- is applicable to \(a\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[STU-collapse: the sheaf left unitor at a pullback image object]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor_image_collapse}
  % NOTE (iter-092 review, lvb minor): the display below orients the tensor comparison as
  % \((\mu_{\mathbf 1,\cdot}).\mathrm{hom}\) in the solved-for-unitor form, but solving the authoritative
  % Lean statement \(\mu.\mathrm{hom};(\varepsilon\rhd);\mathrm{unitor}=a.\mathrm{map}\,\lambda\) for the
  % unitor yields \(\mu.\mathbf{inv}\) in the middle factor, not \(\mu.\mathrm{hom}\). The Lean statement
  % is correct and was formalized; only this paraphrase has the hom/inv slip. Cosmetic; non-blocking.
  \uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime, def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\), write \(a = a_Y = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
  sheafification reflector on \(Y\), \(\varepsilon = \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) for its unit comparison
  and \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) for its tensor comparison, and \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback. For the image object
  \(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\) --- the sheafification of the presheaf pullback
  of \(M\) --- the sheaf-level left unitor collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to the
  sheafified presheaf left unitor:
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}))\bigr).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a.\mathrm{map}\,\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr).
  \]
  This is the shared structural residual of \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} and
  of the central-leg sub-step of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime, def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  The proof is a by-hand presheaf reduction across the sheafification adjunction; it does \emph{not}
  use the localization-monoidal API. The genuine content is a tensor-bifunctoriality plus
  adjunction-triangle collapse over the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(\mathbf 1)\) / \(\mathbf 1_{\mathtt{Functor}}\)
  defeq seam, not a definitional pass-through.

  \emph{Step 1: single-tensor presentation.} Present
  \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) by its single-tensor form
  (\cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime}, \cref{def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso}):
  \(\mu.\mathrm{hom} = a.\mathrm{map}(\eta_{\mathbf 1} \otimes \eta_P)\), where
  \(\eta = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}).\mathrm{unit}\) is the sheafification unit and \(a\) the
  reflector; and expand \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\) at the image object as the sheafified
  presheaf left unitor (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}). All factors are kept inside the one
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) monoidal instance on the \(\circ\,\mathtt{forget}_2\) carrier.

  \emph{Step 2: presheaf collapse identity.} Reduce the goal to the pure presheaf equation
  \[
    (\eta_{\mathbf 1} \otimes \eta_P) \mathbin{;} (\varepsilon \otimes \mathbf 1) \mathbin{;} \lambda_{aP}
      = \lambda_P \mathbin{;} \eta_P ,
  \]
  obtained from tensor bifunctoriality (the interchange law
  \((f_1\otimes f_2)\circ(g_1\otimes g_2) = (f_1\circ g_1)\otimes(f_2\circ g_2)\)), the
  sheafification-adjunction \emph{right-triangle} \(\eta_{\mathbf 1}\mathbin{;}\varepsilon = \mathbf 1\)
  on the unit factor, and presheaf left-unitor naturality of \(\eta_P\).

  \emph{Step 3: merge the functor-image legs and cancel the counit.} Both sides are
  \(a.\mathrm{map}\)-images; merge them by functoriality \(F(g)\circ F(h)=F(g\circ h)\), apply the
  Step-2 identity inside \(a.\mathrm{map}\), and cancel the trailing counit by the sheafification
  adjunction \emph{left-triangle}. This leaves exactly the displayed equation. The merge, the
  triangle rewrites, and the unit-factor whisker are all forced to be term-mode
  (\(\mathtt{congrArg}_2\)/\(\mathtt{Eq.trans}\)) because the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(\mathbf 1)\)
  wrapper on \(\eta\)'s codomain makes the connecting objects defeq-but-not-syntactic, so
  \(\mathtt{rw}\)/\(\mathtt{erw}\) miss or \(\mathtt{whnf}\)-bomb.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[Cone A sub-lemma 2: the pullback-object left unitor is the sheafified presheaf unitor]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse, def:pullback_val_iso}
  With notation as above, write \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\) and let
  \[
    W_{\lambda}
      = (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
  \]
  be the \emph{unit-reconciled} \((\mathbf 1,\,f^{*}M)\)-wrapper: its tensor unit is the presheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1 = \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\) and its left tensor leg is the unit comparison
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) (\emph{not} the \(\delta\)-wrapper \(W\) of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}, whose unit is \(F\,\mathbf 1\) and whose
  left leg is \(\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)). Post-composed with the sheaf-level left unitor at the
  pullback object \(f^{*}M\), \(W_{\lambda}\) equals the sheafified presheaf left unitor of
  \(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\), conjugated by \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) on the target:
  \[
    (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(f^{*}M)).\mathrm{hom}
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1 = \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\) is the presheaf tensor unit. The unit-reconciled
  wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\) is precisely the right-hand tail produced by the
  \(\delta\)-side \(\eta\)-whisker identity
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}; that lemma performs the
  \(W \to W_{\lambda}\) handoff that converts the \(\delta\)-wrapper into the unit-reconciled wrapper
  consumed here.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse, def:pullback_val_iso}
  \leanok
  Split the wrapper's tensor leg \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M))\)
  by functoriality of the sheaf tensor into the unit-factor whisker
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \rhd (-)\) followed by the right-factor whisker
  \((-) \lhd (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\). Naturality of the sheaf-level left unitor against the
  \(\mathtt{pbv}\,M\)-leg (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality}) slides that right-factor whisker
  past the unitor, producing the trailing \((\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom}\) on the right-hand side and
  reducing the unitor to its value at the image object
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\). The residual --- the
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) reconciliation \(\mu\) and the \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\)
  whisker \(\varepsilon\) composed against the sheaf left unitor at that image object --- is exactly the
  localization-image collapse \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}: the sheaf left unitor at
  the image object collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}})\). Cancelling the
  \(\varepsilon\)-whisker against the collapse's \(\varepsilon^{-1}\)-whisker leaves the displayed
  identity. No new sheaf-level monoidal structure is introduced --- only unitor naturality and the
  image-collapse \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}, whose own proof is the by-hand
  bifunctoriality + adjunction-triangle reduction recorded there.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[Cone A sub-lemma 3: the whiskered unit comparison is the sheafified \(\eta\)-whisker]
  \label{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}
  \uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso_eq_sheafify_eta, lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural, def:pullback_val_iso}
  With notation as above, write \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\),
  \(\eta\,F = \mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\) for the presheaf
  oplax unit (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}), and let
  \begin{align*}
    W &= (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X)\otimes(\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr),\\
    W_{\lambda} &= (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\otimes(\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
  \end{align*}
  be respectively the \(\delta\)-identification right wrapper of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta} (tensor unit \(F\,\mathbf 1\), left leg
  \(\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)) and the unit-reconciled wrapper of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} (tensor unit \(\mathbf 1 =
  \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\), left leg \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\)). Then the wrapper \(W\),
  post-composed with the unit comparison whiskered into the left tensor factor, equals the sheafified
  presheaf \(\eta\)-whisker followed by the unit-reconciled wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\):
  \[
    W \mathbin{;}
    (\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f)\,\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}).\mathrm{hom}
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} W_{\lambda}.
  \]
  This is the \(W \to W_{\lambda}\) handoff: it converts the \(\delta\)-wrapper into the unit-reconciled
  wrapper consumed by \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify}, at the cost of the
  \(\eta\)-whisker prefix.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso_eq_sheafify_eta, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta, lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural, def:pullback_val_iso}
  \leanok
  The proof composes two reconciliations.

  \emph{Bridge-1 substitution and whisker expansion.} The whisker
  \((\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f)\,\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}).\mathrm{hom}\) is the
  bifunctorial action of the sheaf tensor on \((\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\) and the
  identity. Substituting bridge~1 (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_eq_sheafify_eta}),
  \((\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom} = (\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}
  \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\), and
  splitting by functoriality of the tensor product expresses the whisker as the three whiskered legs
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}\), \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F)\),
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\), each whiskered by \(\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}\) into the left
  factor.

  \emph{Central-leg STU naturality.} The central leg --- \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F)\) whiskered by
  \(f^{*}M\), transported across the tensor reconciliation \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\)
  identifying the sheaf tensor with the sheafification of the presheaf tensor
  \(F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1 \otimes F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) --- becomes the bare sheafified
  presheaf \(\eta\)-whisker \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\). This is
  \emph{not} a definitional pass-through: it is exactly the argument-1 specialization, at
  \(q = \mathbf 1_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\), of the \emph{already-proven} two-argument
  naturality \cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural} of \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\):
  \[
    a_Y.\mathrm{map}(p \otimes \mathbf 1) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{STU}(P',Q)
      = \mathtt{STU}(P,Q) \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl((a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,p)_{\mathrm{val}} \otimes \mathbf 1\bigr),
  \]
  instantiated at \(p = \eta\,F\). The general lemma's right-hand whisker
  \((a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathbf 1))_{\mathrm{val}}\) collapses to the identity by
  \(\mathtt{Functor.map\_id}\) / \(\mathtt{tensorHom\_id}\), so no new coherence core has to be built ---
  the proven section-level naturality discharges this leg directly.

  \emph{Left-factor device cancellation.} The flanking legs
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}\) and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\)
  introduced by the substitution, whiskered into the left factor, recombine with the wrapper \(W\). The
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-component of \(W\)'s tensor leg is inverse to
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}\), so it cancels and rewrites \(W\)'s tensor unit
  \(F\,\mathbf 1\) to the presheaf unit \(\mathbf 1\) and its left leg to \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\),
  i.e. converts \(W\) into the unit-reconciled wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}).
  Reassociating exposes the cancelling pairs (\(\mathtt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id}\)/\(\mathtt{inv\_hom\_id}\)),
  and the composite is exactly \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
  \mathbin{;} W_{\lambda}\), the displayed identity. This is the unit-side analogue of the whiskering
  for the unit pair in \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}, now retaining the whisker rather than
  discarding it as an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\) and splitting by functoriality gives a three-leg identity with right-hand side
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(F.\mathrm{map}\,\lambda_{M_{\mathrm{val}}})\). Rewrite each leg by its
  reconciliation: the \(\delta\)-leg to \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X\,M\) by
  \(\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta}\) (which also pins the shared right wrapper), the
  \(\eta\)-whisker leg to the whiskered \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) by sub-lemma~3
  (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}), the \(\lambda\)-leg to
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(f^{*}M)\) by sub-lemma~2
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify}), and the right-hand side to the
  \(f^{*}\)-image of the sheaf left unitor via the assembly-form reconciliation --- the
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)-headed naturality of \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) ---
  covered by sub-lemma~1 (\cref{lem:pullback_val_iso_naturality_left_unitor}). The

\begin{lemma}



\leanok
[S4b: unit left-unitor commutes with further restriction]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the unit-tensor contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\) (the left unitor at the
  unit, \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) commutes with further restriction along \(j\): modulo the
  reindexing \(\varrho\),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\ \text{over}\ U)
      \;=\; \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\ \text{over}\ V.
  \]
  Unlike the tensor-restriction comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\), the contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) is \emph{not} built from the abstract pullback: by definition it
  is the \emph{sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor at the unit}, followed by the
  sheafification-adjunction counit (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}). Bridge B1 does not apply to the
  contraction directly; instead the square reduces, via the chart comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\), to a pullback-world left-unitality coherence (Cone~A,
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality}), established below.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, def:unitrestrictiso, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \leanok
  This is the \emph{unit} companion of S2, routed through \emph{Cone~A}. Because the unit object is
  fixed --- there is no module argument --- the square has \emph{no} naturality-in-\(M\) component,
  which makes it strictly simpler than B1.

  \emph{Peeling the contraction.} Write
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} = \mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\lambda_{\mathbf 1})
  \mathbin{;} \varepsilon\), where \(\lambda_{\mathbf 1}\) is the presheaf-level left unitor of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) at the tensor unit \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}\) and \(\varepsilon\) is
  the sheafification-adjunction counit. By \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor} the
  contraction is the sheaf-level left unitor \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\)
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) evaluated at \(M = \mathcal{O}\); restriction along \(j\) commutes
  through the outer \(\mathtt{Functor.mapIso}\)/\(\mathtt{toSheafify}\)/counit legs by functoriality
  and naturality of the unitor (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality}), so the square reduces to
  a single \emph{inner} seam.

  \emph{Inner seam (Cone~A).} The inner seam is the pullback-world left-unitor coherence at the chart
  unit, \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner}. Crucially, this is \emph{not} a missing
  monoidal instance: the presheaf change-of-rings pullback \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) is
  \emph{already} oplax monoidal (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}, via
  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\)), so its left-unitality coherence
  \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left\_unitality\_hom}\) holds for free. The inner seam is the
  \emph{sheafification transport} of that coherence to the sheaf-level maps
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) (sheafified \(\delta\)) and \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\) (sheafified
  \(\eta\)); it is discharged by the sheaf-level left unitality
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality} at \(M = \mathcal{O}_U\), through the chart comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j\) and the unit conjugator \(\mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\)
  (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}). The pentagon plays no role.

  \emph{Outer seam (sheafify and counit).} The remaining legs are formal. The sheafification functor
  acts on the inner unitor square by \(\mathtt{Functor.mapIso}\), and the sheafification unit
  \(\mathtt{toSheafify}\) is natural, so the sheafified squares commute whenever the inner one does.
  The counit \(\varepsilon\) of the sheafification adjunction is a natural transformation, hence its
  naturality square against the restriction-induced morphism is free. Composing the inner agreement
  with these two formal squares yields the equality of the two contractions.

  \emph{Reindexing.} Finally the reindexing \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\)
  (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) threads the equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) through both flanks, absorbing
  the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\) congruence exactly as in S2, and giving the displayed
  identity. No sheaf-level monoidal structure is involved: the structure used lives at the presheaf
  level and is transported through sheafification.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[RFIP pseudonaturality across an equality of open immersions]
  \label{lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_congr_hom}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback_congr_hom}
  \uses{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f, f' : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be open immersions with \(h : f = f'\), and let
  \(A \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The restriction--pullback comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) is pseudonatural
  across the equality \(h\): the comparison at \(f\) followed by the \(\mathtt{pullbackCongr}\,h\)
  reindex equals the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,h\) reindex followed by the comparison at
  \(f'\),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullbackCongr}\,h).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A
    = (\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,h).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f').\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A.
  \]
  This is the leaf reindex helper threaded by the Seam-1 keystone
  (\cref{lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict}) to push the leading comparison onto the literal
  composite chart morphism.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}
  \leanok
  Substituting \(h\) (\(\mathtt{subst}\)) identifies \(f'\) with \(f\) and collapses both congruence
  reindexings \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,h\) and \(\mathtt{pullbackCongr}\,h\) to the identity:
  each is an \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-reindex along a reflexivity equality, forced to be the identity by
  thin-poset uniqueness of the underlying opens-morphisms. Both sides reduce to
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A\) by the identity laws.
\end{proof}

  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}

  \(c = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\) for the \(U\)-built dual chain and
  \[
    c_V = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V
      \mathbin{;} \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M)\bigr)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V
  \]
  for its \(V\)-built refinement (with \(e^M\) replaced by \(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\)).
  Writing \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L} = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\) and
  \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\), the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of the dual chain is its
  \(V\)-built refinement conjugated by \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}\) and \(u_j\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,c
      = \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} c_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat, lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural, lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat, lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict, lem:dualisoofiso_trans, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}
  \leanok
  As \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\) is a functor it carries the composite \(c\) to the composite of the
  \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-images of its three legs; identify each in turn (abbreviating
  \(D_j(X) = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,X\)).
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Leg 1}, \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U\): S3
      (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}) reads
      \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V = \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U)
        \mathbin{;} D_j(L|_{\iota_U}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_L\);
      solving for the middle factor gives
      \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U)
        = \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V
          \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_L)^{-1} \mathbin{;} D_j(L|_{\iota_U})^{-1}\).
    \item \emph{Leg 2}, \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\): module-naturality of
      \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) (T2, \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural}) reads
      \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)
        = D_j(\mathcal O_U) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M)
          \mathbin{;} D_j(L|_{\iota_U})^{-1}\);
      taking inverses, the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of leg 2 is
      \(D_j(L|_{\iota_U}) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M))^{-1}
        \mathbin{;} D_j(\mathcal O_U)^{-1}\).
    \item \emph{Leg 3}, \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\): S4a
      (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat}) reads
      \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V = \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j \mathbin{;} D_j(\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U \mathbin{;} u_j\);
      solving gives \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U
        = D_j(\mathbf 1_U) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}
          \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}\).
  \end{itemize}
  Composing the three legs in order, the two internal \(D_j\) comparisons cancel telescopically: the
  tail \(D_j(L|_{\iota_U})^{-1}\) of leg 1 meets the head \(D_j(L|_{\iota_U})\) of leg 2, and the tail
  \(D_j(\mathcal O_U)^{-1}\) of leg 2 meets the head \(D_j(\mathbf 1_U) = D_j(\mathcal O_U)\) of leg 3
  (the structure-sheaf unit \(\mathbf 1_U\) being \(\mathcal O_U\)). What survives is
  \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V \mathbin{;} (\ast)
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}\), where the middle factor
  \((\ast) = (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_L)^{-1} \mathbin{;}
  (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M))^{-1} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}\)
  is, by contravariant functoriality of \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans},
  \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}),
  \(\bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\varrho_L \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M \mathbin{;} u_j)\bigr)^{-1}\).
  Since \(\varrho_L \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M \mathbin{;} u_j
    = \mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\) is exactly the Seam-1 keystone
  (\cref{lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict}), \((\ast)\) is the middle leg
  \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M))^{-1}\) of \(c_V\), and the
  surviving expression is \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} c_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}\) as claimed.
\end{proof}
